commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=24ba9891c6c03d1239a4647ca3ce164d40c24ada)
commands.review_summary:
The PR widens Telegram text delivery to return `undefined` for empty-text or HTML-whitespace sends and adds a colocated unit test for the new skip behavior.

PR surface: Source +21, Tests +141. Total +162 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source inspection gives a high-confidence reproduction path: current main throws on empty formatted/plain fallback and rethrows empty-text Telegram 400s when no fallback exists. I did not run a live Telegram repro in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The PR needs contributor or maintainer follow-up for live Telegram proof plus a code change to avoid marking skipped sends as delivered; the missing proof gate makes this human-review work rather than a repair marker.

target.title: fix(telegram): silently skip empty-text and HTML-whitespace sends
target.body:
Supersedes #59725. The original PR was auto-closed on 2026-04-02 by openclaw-barnacle for the 10-open-PR cap. Reopening it programmatically fails because the close `stateReason` is `COMPLETED` plus the branch was force-pushed since, so this is a fresh PR carrying the rebased fix. ## Behavior addressed Interrupted mid-reply turns can stream bare `
`, `
`, `
`, or ` ` chunks. The Telegram Bot API rejects these with HTTP 400 (`message text is empty` legacy, `text must be non-empty` newer wording). Previously this surfaced as a user-visible delivery failure; this PR silently skips the send instead. ## Change One commit (`24ba9891c6`) touches `extensions/telegram/src/bot/delivery.send.ts` plus a new colocated unit test. - `sendTelegramText` return type widened to `Promise`. Both call sites in `delivery.replies.ts` already handle `undefined` via `if (firstDeliveredMessageId == null)`. - Pre-flight branch: when formatted HTML trims to empty AND `plainText` trims to empty, log via `logVerbose` and return `undefined`. - Post-call branch: when Telegram replies with the empty-text 400 AND there is no plain fallback, log via `logVerbose` and return `undefined`. - `EMPTY_TEXT_ERR_RE` now matches both `message text is empty` and `text must be non-empty`. - All other Telegram client rejections still throw unchanged. Non-empty payloads still deliver via the existing markdown / plain-fallback pipeline. The new test file (`extensions/telegram/src/bot/delivery.send.test.ts`, 10 cases) was verified to fail on unpatched `upstream/main` (8 of 10 assertions fail with pre-fix throw / 400 surface) and pass after the patch. ## Verification ``` Real environment tested: vitest unit harness against extensions/telegram/src/bot/delivery.send.ts with a mocked grammY Bot.api.sendMessage that throws Telegram's 400 empty-text descriptions Behavior addressed: Telegram send API call returning 400 (or sendTelegramText throwing) when an interrupted mid-reply turn streams bare
,
,
, or   chunks; now silently skipped with logVerbose Exact steps or command run after this patch: node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/telegram/src/bot/delivery.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot/delivery.send.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/send.test.ts Evidence after fix: Test Files 3 passed (3) | Tests 173 passed (173) | Start at 18:28:18 | Duration 4.40s Observed result after fix: For all six empty-HTML payload shapes (` `, `
`, `
`, `
`, ` `, `   `) sendTelegramText returns undefined; for ` ` the pre-flight skip fires with zero API calls, for bare-
and   shapes Telegram is called once, the 400 is caught, and the silent skip fires; newer "text must be non-empty" wording is also caught; unrelated 400s (e.g. "chat not found") still throw; well-formed payloads still deliver What was not tested: real Telegram round-trip with a live bot token (mocked grammY client reproduces the post-strip 400 deterministically) ``` Build: `pnpm build` -> 126.3s, no errors.
target.author: amittell
